comment: Finite-use scout cards. Each scout card starts with scout_card_max_pips (3) pips and decrements by 1 on each deploy (a deploy = applying the scout card to a player on the roster). When pips hit 0 the scout card is RETIRED — permanently exhausted, no recharge ever. To deploy the same scout card again the kid must own another copy (each copy carries its own 3 pips, so owning N copies = 3N total deploys). Energy is now a pure sink mechanic: scan or earn scout cards to maintain a deck. Stefan 2026-05-03 (FINAL — replaces both v2 single-pip and v3 three-pip-with-recharge models).

ui.retired_help: This scout card is exhausted — scan or earn another to deploy again.

deprecated_fields.comment: All time-based recharge knobs removed under v4. The unprefixed card_max_pips key was renamed to scout_card_max_pips to match Stefan's terminology (the collectibles are SCOUT cards, not player cards).

deprecated_models.comment: Original ship: 3 pips, 48h cooldown between every deploy, manual recharge item required when all pips spent.
Brief intermediate (2026-05-03 morning): 1 pip per scout card with 24h auto-recharge. Replaced same day.
Brief intermediate (2026-05-03 midday): 3 pips with a single 24h full-reset only after all pips spent. Replaced same day after Stefan clarified the intent was finite-use, no recharge ever.

roster_build_energy_budget.comment: The 8-Energy/week roster-build budget (sum of energy_cost across scout cards in a roster) is removed. Pips are the only stamina/scarcity model — energy_cost on scout card templates is retained for back-compat but no longer validated at roster lock.

duplicates.comment: Each owned copy of a scout card carries its own pips_remaining. Two copies of the same scout card = two independent counters of 3 pips each = 6 total deploys of that scout card. The collection screen renders each copy as its own tile (or a stacked tile with a quantity badge — see CollectionScreen).
